LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CARMEN ECHAURI'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS AND FOR SANCTIONS - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. PERSONNEL RECORDS COME WITHIN THE ZONE OF PRIVACY PROTECTED BY ARTICLE I, 1 OF THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION. SEE BOARD OF TRUSTEES V. SUPERIOR COURT (1981) 119 CA3D 516. THE PRIVILEGE IS NOT ABSOLUTE. WHEN AN INDIVIDUAL'S RIGHT OF PRIVACY IN HIS PERSONAL AFFAIRS CONFLICTS WITH THE PUBLIC NEED FOR DISCOVERY IN LITIGATION, THE COMPETING INTERESTS MUST BE CAREFULLY BALANCED. SEE BRITT V. SUPERIOR COURT (1978) 20 C.3D 391. DEFENDANT HAS MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S PAST JOB PERFORMANCE IN THESE PRIOR EMPLOYMENT RECORDS IS DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE LITIGATION AND ESSENTIAL TO THE RESOLUTION OF THIS LAWSUIT, SUCH THAT DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO THIS DISCOVERY OUTWEIGHS PLAINTIFF'S PRIVACY RIGHTS IN THE INFORMATION SOUGHT. THE SUBPOENAED DOCUMENTS ARE TO BE PRODUCED UNDER A PROTECTIVE ORDER FOR "ATTORNEY'S EYES ONLY." REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Motion To Compel Psychiatric Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Apr-10-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. LAWRENCE A. ORGAN of California Civil Rights Law Group, Tel No. (415) 453-4740 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MAYA OHANA of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and takes DEFENDANT EKO VENTURE, INC.'S Motion To Compel Psychiatric Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff off calendar at this time. Pursuant to the stipulation entered by the Plaintiff, Plaintiff is not seeking ongoing emotional distress damages, thus cutting off damages as of January 31, 2015. Any issues regarding a treating psychologist testifying at trial for the Plaintiff shall be deferred to the trial judge. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL MAY SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR I.M.E. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)

Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Response To Req.S For Production, Set Two And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL A. LEVY of The Armstrong Law Firm, Tel No. (415) 928-1293 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MARCIE FITZSIMMONS of Gordin & Rees, LLP, Tel No. (415) 875-4131 appearing fot the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CARMEN DIAZ ECHAURI'S Amended Motion To Compel Defendant EkoVenture, Inc.'s Further Response To Requests For Production, Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. The timeline in question is classic attorney work product. It was created by the defense attorney based on her review of the file. She avers that the timeline also contains her written impressions and notes. Where there is no question as to whether the subject is attorney work product the Court is statutorily denied the power to order its production for in camera review. (See Costco Wholesale Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 725, 739.) Further, the deposition testimony reflects an immediate objection to any testimony regarding the document, which precludes a finding of waiver. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions in the amount of $2,940 to Defendant within 30 days from entry of this Order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, Tel No. (415) 828-1919 (302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Response To Req.'S For Production, Set Two; And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Continued To Nov-04-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF CARMEN ECHAURI's Motion To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions - Plaintiff's motion to quash is denied. Personnel records come within the zone of privacy protected by Article I, 1 of the California Constitution. See Board of Trustees v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA3d 516. The privilege is not absolute. When an individual's right of privacy in his personal affairs conflicts with the public need for discovery in litigation, the competing interests must be carefully balanced. See Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 C.3d 391. Defendant has made a sufficient showing that the discovery sought regarding plaintiff's past job performance in these prior employment records is directly relevant to the subject matter of the litigation and essential to the resolution of this lawsuit, such that defendant's right to this discovery outweighs plaintiff's privacy rights in the information sought. The subpoenaed documents are to be produced under a protective order for "attorney's eyes only." Requests for sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm
